desc: Tamraght is a surf-oriented Berber village in Taghazout Bay with relaxed cafés, yoga camps, and quick access to multiple Atlantic breaks while staying quieter than central Taghazout.
hls: Multiple beginner-to-intermediate surf breaks within short taxi distance
Lower-key village atmosphere than Taghazout centre
Strong surf-camp + yoga stay ecosystem for medium-length trips
Fast airport transfers from Agadir compared with most Morocco surf bases

todo.name: Imourane morning surf lesson
todo.desc: Book early for cleaner wind windows and easier beginner coaching slots.

todo.name: Banana Beach sunset walk
todo.desc: Use late afternoon light for a long shoreline walk south of the village.

todo.name: Paradise Valley half-day loop
todo.desc: Check water levels first, then combine pools, canyon views, and roadside fruit stops.

todo.name: Tamraght café work block
todo.desc: Mix Moroccan breakfasts with laptop-friendly café sessions between tides.

todo.name: Agadir souk evening run
todo.desc: Head to Souk El Had before closing for spices, textiles, and local snacks.

todo.name: Taghazout harbor fish grills
todo.desc: Pair a promenade walk with simple grilled-fish spots near the port.

wifi.note: Many surf camps offer workable Wi-Fi, but speeds vary by occupancy.

prac.fltNote: Tamraght is primarily reached via Agadir (AGA), where UK nonstop coverage is strongest in winter-sun and peak summer weeks; outside those periods, many UK/Ireland routings connect via Casablanca, Lisbon, Madrid, or London. Marrakech (RAK) is the main fallback gateway when AGA schedules thin out, but the added road transfer can materially extend total travel time. Easter, July-August, and Christmas/New Year usually bring the highest airfare and seat-pressure months.
prac.currency: Morocco local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Surf safety changes quickly with tide and swell; confirm beginner-friendly windows with local instructors each day.
Tamraght hills and side streets are dimly lit at night—use a torch/phone light when walking back from beach roads.
Friday prayer-time and weekend traffic can slow Agadir transfers; leave extra margin for airport runs.
ATM availability in village can be patchy during busy weeks, so carry backup cash before late arrivals.

sweet: Oct-Apr for consistent surf swells and mild daytime temperatures.
